task_completion.description: Mark the first occurrence of 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' as a citation with its full citation string.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Mark Citation' dialog box, TA field codes in the document text (e.g., { TA \l "New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, 376 U.S. 254 (1964)" }), or the final Table of Authorities showing the full citation 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, 376 U.S. 254 (1964)'.

task_completion.description: Mark the first occurrence of 'Curtis Publishing Co. v. Butts' as a citation with its full citation string.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Mark Citation' dialog box, TA field codes in the document text, or the final Table of Authorities showing the full citation 'Curtis Publishing Co. v. Butts, 388 U.S. 130 (1967)'.

task_completion.description: Mark the first occurrence of 'Gertz v. Robert Welch, Inc.' as a citation with its full citation string.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Mark Citation' dialog box, TA field codes in the document text, or the final Table of Authorities showing the full citation 'Gertz v. Robert Welch, Inc., 418 U.S. 323 (1974)'.

task_completion.description: Insert a Table of Authorities in the 'TABLE OF AUTHORITIES' section on page 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a generated Table of Authorities block appearing on page 2 of the document under the 'TABLE OF AUTHORITIES' heading.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Table of Authorities uses the 'Classic' format and 'Cases' category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Table of Authorities' insertion dialog showing 'Classic' selected in the Formats dropdown and 'Cases' in the Category dropdown. In the document, the table should have a 'CASES' heading and match the Classic style (typically no dot leaders between the case name and page number).
Look for the 'Save As' dialog showing the file name 'Defamation_Brief_Final.docx' and the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents', or the Word title bar updating to 'Defamation_Brief_Final.docx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Word 2010 ribbon (References tab) and dialog boxes, not terminal commands or python scripts modifying the .docx file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used Word's native citation and TOA features rather than manually typing the table.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Mark Citation' dialog, 'Table of Authorities' dialog, or Word field codes (like { TA } or { TOA }) being generated, rather than the agent simply typing out the case names and page numbers.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using Word's Save As functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Word 'Save As' dialog box being used to create the new file, rather than a file explorer rename operation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The three cases must be marked with their full citation strings: (1) New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, 376 U.S. 254 (1964); (2) Curtis Publishing Co. v. Butts, 388 U.S. 130 (1967); (3) Gertz v. Robert Welch, Inc., 418 U.S. 323 (1974). The Table of Authorities must be generated using the 'Classic' format under the 'Cases' category. The final file must be saved exactly as 'Defamation_Brief_Final.docx'.
